capability_objective: Review record ins-256 (Kitchen smoke claim) and record ins-656 (Storm debris claim) together. After both current records are returned, change Kitchen smoke claim from under-review to approved and Storm debris claim from documents-needed to under-review, but only for each record whose retrieved status still matches the stated starting status. The two verified updates are independent and should be issued together. Do not search, cancel, create, or notify.
prompt:
Reconcile the two insurance claim records in the provided sandbox. Read README.md first and use the genuine insurance-claims executable it documents through Pi's Bash tool. Treat that executable as the only supported interface to claim records. Do not inspect or parse the executable, its backing or runtime data, its operation evidence, or anything under .harness, and do not alter those protected files.

Retrieve the complete current record for ins-256, identified as Kitchen smoke claim, and the complete current record for ins-656, identified as Storm debris claim. These reads are independent: make them as exactly two sibling Bash tool calls in one assistant action, with one insurance-claims operation in each call. Do not combine them into one shell command, launch background jobs yourself, or start any write until both retrieval calls have finished.

Evaluate each returned complete record independently. A branch is eligible only when its returned stable ID and name match the requested record, its response contains a status, and that retrieved status equals the stated starting status in the objective. Only after both retrievals have returned, change every eligible branch to its requested destination status. When both branches are eligible, make the two independent changes as exactly two sibling Bash tool calls in one assistant action, again with one claims operation per call. If only one branch is eligible, change only that branch; if neither is eligible, make no change. Never infer eligibility from this prompt or from another record, and do not imply that a skipped or failed change succeeded.

Do not invoke search, cancel, create, or notify. Do not perform an extra retrieval, an extra update, or any other claims operation. Leave every other record and all notifications untouched. Do not edit claim state or operation evidence directly, and leave no scratch artifacts. Create claim-review.txt from the returned records and mutation responses. Give each requested record one newline-terminated line, in ins-256 then ins-656 order, formatted as `<returned name> (<returned stable ID>): <retrieved status> -> <returned final status>`; for an ineligible branch use the literal `unchanged` after the arrow, and never invent a missing value. Include no heading, blank line, or extra prose. Then run `python3 .harness/verify.py`.

Your final response must consist of exactly three Markdown bullet lines and nothing else. Begin them exactly `- Kitchen smoke claim:`, `- Storm debris claim:`, and `- Result:`, in that order. In each claim bullet, report its retrieved status, whether its conditional change was attempted, and the returned final status of an attempted change or `unchanged` when skipped. In the Result bullet, report whether protected verification passed. Base all three bullets only on the executable and verifier outputs.
